name: Cluster 3: META (Holistic Reviewer Simulation)
description: Simulates real study section review with multi-pass reading and checks cross-cluster coherence
grounding_instructions: Read Cluster 1 (WHAT) and Cluster 2 (HOW) feedback files for context. Check that weaknesses identified by domain critics have been addressed. Verify internal consistency across the full proposal.

criteria.name: Reviewer Perspective
criteria.description: Application of reviewer mindset and critique anticipation via multi-pass simulation
review_passes: 2-minute skim: Read specific aims page only. Can you identify the hypothesis, significance, and innovation within 2 minutes? Is it compelling enough to keep reading?
15-minute read: Skim all sections. Note weaknesses in logic, feasibility concerns, missing controls, unclear methods. What would you flag as a reviewer?
Scoring pass: Assign NIH-style criterion scores for Significance, Innovation, Approach, Investigators, Environment. What overall impact score would this receive?

scores.1: No reviewer perspective applied; obvious critique points missed
scores.2: Minimal reviewer thinking; misses major potential criticisms
scores.3: Some reviewer perspective; anticipates some likely critiques
scores.4: Good reviewer simulation; identifies most likely concerns across all three passes
scores.5: Excellent reviewer perspective; anticipates and addresses all likely criticisms with specific remediation

criteria.name: Cross-Cluster Coherence
criteria.description: Alignment between conceptual foundation (WHAT) and technical approach (HOW)

scores.1: Major disconnects between hypothesis, methods, and aims; proposal reads as if sections were written independently
scores.2: Significant gaps: innovation claims unsupported by methods, or aims don't test the stated hypothesis
scores.3: Adequate coherence but some logical gaps remain between what is claimed and what is proposed
scores.4: Good coherence: hypothesis, methods, and aims align well with minor inconsistencies
scores.5: Excellent coherence: all elements reinforce each other seamlessly; innovation claims are directly supported by the experimental design
